summary: MGRS coordinate conversion is implemented but likely failing due to missing GPS data from backend API

backend_investigation.api_response_check: Failed - no GPS data in response
backend_investigation.spectrum_analyzer_status: Returns spectrum data, not GPS data
backend_investigation.missing_component: GPS data provider not implemented in backend

root_cause.primary: Backend /api/status endpoint does not provide GPS data
details: The frontend MGRS conversion code is correct and properly implemented
The mgrs.js library is loaded and accessible in the browser
The API endpoint returns spectrum analyzer status, not system status with GPS
No GPS data provider service found in the backend implementation

recommendations: Implement GPS data retrieval in backend server.js
Add GPSD connection to fetch real-time GPS coordinates
Modify /api/status endpoint to include GPS data structure
Consider using node-gpsd package (already in package.json dependencies)
Ensure GPS data format matches frontend expectations

test_scenarios.manual_test: Open browser console and run: latLonToMGRS(40.7128, -74.0060)
test_scenarios.current_result: Function exists but no GPS data to convert

conclusion: MGRS conversion is fully implemented in the frontend but the backend is not providing GPS data. The issue is not with the MGRS library or conversion logic, but with the missing GPS data source in the API response.
